meaning: n.(一)步,速度,步调,步法,步态vi.踱步,缓慢的走vt.用步测,踱步于,(马)溜花蹄
examples: They set off at a steady pace.
Congestion frequently reduces traffic to walking pace.
We set off at a smartish pace.
That stems from our kind of island life, our pace of the way we live.
Peter Pace as the nation's top military officer and the senior military adviser to the president.
